# README

# Relational Data Modeler ![logo](reference/figures/ReDaMoR.png)

[![CRAN_Status_Badge](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/ReDaMoR)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=ReDaMoR)
[![](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/ReDaMoR)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=ReDaMoR)

The [ReDaMoR](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/) package allows the
manipulation of relational data models in R. It provides functions to
create, import and save relational data models. These functions are
accessible through a graphical user interface made with
[Shiny](https://shiny.posit.co/).

A public instance of the Shiny app is available
[here](https://pgodard.shinyapps.io/ReDaMoR).

The main features of ReDaMoR are the following:

- Create data model from scratch with a graphical user interface
- Save data model in a json format
- Import SQL data model generated with [MySQL
  Workbench](https://www.mysql.com/products/workbench/)
- Document data types as R types (character, numeric, logical, …)
- Add comments to tables and fields to improve model documentation
- Check and auto-correct model constraints (types, keys, uniqueness,
  mandatory fields)
- Confront data to the model to check their compatibility
- Support of matrices (\>= 0.5.0)

The [TKCat](https://patzaw.github.io/TKCat/) package relies on ReDaMoR
to facilitate the management of data from knowledge resources which are
manipulated as modeled database (MDB) objects.

The package can also be tested in [Rstudio
Cloud](https://rstudio.cloud/project/1033803).

``` r

library(ReDaMoR)
m <- model_relational_data()
```

You can also run the code documented in the [package
vignette](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/articles/ReDaMoR.html).

# Introduction

In R, data are often stored in data frames which are tables in which
each row represents a record and each column a variable. Because data
frames are highly used they have been improved in different objects such
as [tibble](https://tibble.tidyverse.org/),
[data.table](https://rdatatable.gitlab.io/data.table/) or
[AnnotatedDataFrame](https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/html/Biobase.html).

However, in many projects, the data do not fit in only one table but
they are organized in different data frames, each of them dealing with a
specific concept. These tables are often related to each other by some
variables. Depending on the diversity of the concepts, it can be
difficult to remember what these tables represent and how they are
connected. Fortunately, because they are tables, a set of data frames
can be directly documented using a relational data model.

The [datamodelr](https://github.com/bergant/datamodelr) R package
provides tools to document relational data. The generate data models are
leveraged by the [dm](https://github.com/cynkra/dm) R package to
interact more easily with relational data.

Here we present the ReDaMoR package which also allows the manipulation
of relational data models in R but with an approach quite different from
the one implemented in datamodelr. It provides functions to create,
import and save relational data models. These functions are accessible
through a graphical user interface made with
[Shiny](https://shiny.posit.co/).

The main features of ReDaMoR are the following:

- Create data model from scratch with a graphical user interface
- Save data model in a json format
- Import SQL data model generated with [MySQL
  Workbench](https://www.mysql.com/products/workbench/)
- Document data types as R types (character, numeric, logical, …)
- Add comments to tables and fields to improve model documentation
- Check and auto-correct model constraints (types, keys, uniqueness,
  mandatory fields)
- Confront data to the model to check their compatibility
- Support of matrices (\>= 0.5.0)

The [TKCat](https://patzaw.github.io/TKCat/) package relies on ReDaMoR
to facilitate the management of data from knowledge resources which are
manipulated as modeled database (MDB) objects.

# Installation

## From CRAN

``` r

install.packages("ReDaMoR")
```

## Dependencies

The following R packages available on CRAN are required:

- [dplyr](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=dplyr): A Grammar of Data
  Manipulation
- [magrittr](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=magrittr): A
  Forward-Pipe Operator for R
- [visNetwork](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=visNetwork): Network
  Visualization using ‘vis.js’ Library
- [readr](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=readr): Read Rectangular
  Text Data
- [shiny](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=shiny): Web Application
  Framework for R
- [shinyjs](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=shinyjs): Easily Improve
  the User Experience of Your Shiny Apps in Seconds
- [jsonlite](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=jsonlite): A Simple and
  Robust JSON Parser and Generator for R
- [DT](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=DT): A Wrapper of the
  JavaScript Library ‘DataTables’
- [colourpicker](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=colourpicker): A
  Colour Picker Tool for Shiny and for Selecting Colours in Plots
- [rintrojs](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=rintrojs): Wrapper for
  the ‘Intro.js’ Library
- [markdown](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=markdown): Render
  Markdown with ‘commonmark’
- [rstudioapi](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=rstudioapi): Safely
  Access the RStudio API
- [crayon](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=crayon): Colored Terminal
  Output
- [utils](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=utils): The R Utils Package
- [graphics](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=graphics): The R
  Graphics Package
- [stats](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=stats): The R Stats Package
- [Matrix](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=Matrix): Sparse and Dense
  Matrix Classes and Methods

And those are suggested:

- [knitr](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=knitr): A General-Purpose
  Package for Dynamic Report Generation in R
- [rmarkdown](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=rmarkdown): Dynamic
  Documents for R
- [igraph](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=igraph): Network Analysis
  and Visualization
- [base64enc](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=base64enc): Tools for
  ‘base64’ Encoding

## From github

``` r

devtools::install_github("patzaw/ReDaMoR")
```

# Documentation

Documentation is available in [this
vignette](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/articles/ReDaMoR.html).

# TKCat: Tailored Knowledge Catalog

The aim of [TKCat](https://patzaw.github.io/TKCat/) (Tailored Knowledge
Catalog) is to facilitate the management of data from knowledge
resources that are frequently used alone or together in research
environments. In TKCat, knowledge resources are manipulated as modeled
database (MDB) objects. These objects provide access to the data tables
along with a general description of the resource and a detail data model
generated with ReDaMoR documenting the tables, their fields and their
relationships. These MDB are then gathered in catalogs that can be
easily explored an shared. TKCat provides tools to easily subset, filter
and combine MDBs and create new catalogs suited for specific needs.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`RelDataModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/RelDataModel.md)
  : Create a RelDataModel object
- [`RelTableModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/RelTableModel.md)
  : Create a RelTableModel object
- [`SUPPTYPES`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/SUPPTYPES.md)
  : Supported R types
- [`add_field()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/add_field.md)
  : Add a field to a table in a RelDataModel
- [`add_foreign_key()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/add_foreign_key.md)
  : Add a foreign key between two tables
- [`add_index()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/add_index.md)
  : Add an index to a table in a RelDataModel
- [`add_table()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/add_table.md)
  : Add a table to a RelDataModel
- [`as_type()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/as_type.md) :
  Convert an object into a specific type
- [`auto_layout()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/auto_layout.md)
  : Pre-compute RelDataModel layout when missing any x or y table
  position
- [`c(`*`<RelDataModel>`*`)`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/c.RelDataModel.md)
  : Merge RelDataModel objects
- [`check_foreign_keys()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/check_foreign_keys.md)
  : Check the availability of foreign keys
- [`check_types()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/check_types.md)
  : Check if a set of types is supported
- [`clean_autosaved_RelDataModels()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/clean_autosaved_RelDataModels.md)
  : Remove all autosaved RelDataModel
- [`col_types()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/col_types.md)
  : Get the types of the columns of a RelTableModel object
- [`confront_data()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/confront_data.md)
  : Confront a RelDataModel to actual data
- [`confront_table_data()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/confront_table_data.md)
  : Confront a RelTableModel to actual data
- [`conv_type_ref()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/conv_type_ref.md)
  : Convert a set of types from or to R supported types
- [`copy_fields()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/copy_fields.md)
  : Copy fields from one table to another in a RelDataModel
- [`correct_constraints()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/correct_constraints.md)
  : Correct the constraints of a table to make them consistent
- [`df_to_model()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/df_to_model.md)
  : Create a RelDataModel object from column names of data frames
- [`format(`*`<RelTableModel>`*`)`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/format.RelTableModel.md)
  : Format a RelTableModel object for printing
- [`format_confrontation_report()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/format_confrontation_report.md)
  : Format confrontation report for printing in console
- [`format_confrontation_report_md()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/format_confrontation_report_md.md)
  : Format confrontation report in markdown format
- [`fromDBM()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/fromDBM.md) :
  Convert a list of 5 normalized tibbles in a RelDataModel object
- [`get_foreign_keys()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/get_foreign_keys.md)
  : Get a foreign key table from an object
- [`get_foreign_keys(`*`<RelDataModel>`*`)`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/get_foreign_keys.RelDataModel.md)
  : Get foreign keys in RelDataModel
- [`get_foreign_keys(`*`<RelTableModel>`*`)`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/get_foreign_keys.RelTableModel.md)
  : Get foreign keys from RelTableModel
- [`guess_constraints()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/guess_constraints.md)
  : Guess RelDataModel constraints based on the provided or existing
  tables
- [`identical_RelDataModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/identical_RelDataModel.md)
  : Check if two RelDataModel are identical
- [`identical_RelTableModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/identical_RelTableModel.md)
  : Check if two RelTableModel are identical
- [`index_table()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/index_table.md)
  : List indexes of a RelTableModel object
- [`is.MatrixModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/is.MatrixModel.md)
  : Check if the object is a RelTableModel matrix object
- [`is.RelDataModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/is.RelDataModel.md)
  : Check if the object is a RelDataModel object
- [`is.RelTableModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/is.RelTableModel.md)
  : Check if the object is a RelTableModel object
- [`is_MM()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/is_MM.md) :
  Identify if a file is in MatrixMarket text format
- [`lengths()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/lengths.md) :
  Lengths of object elements
- [`list_autosaved_RelDataModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/list_autosaved_RelDataModel.md)
  : List autosaved RelDataModel
- [`list_type_ref()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/list_type_ref.md)
  : List supported types references
- [`modelToVn()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/modelToVn.md)
  : VisNetwork representation of a RelDataModel object
- [`model_relational_data()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/model_relational_data.md)
  : Relational data modeler GUI
- [`norm_type_ref()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/norm_type_ref.md)
  : Normalize type names
- [`order_fields()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/order_fields.md)
  : Order fields in a table in a RelDataModel
- [`order_indexes()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/order_indexes.md)
  : Order index in a table in a RelDataModel
- [`plot(`*`<RelDataModel>`*`)`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/plot.RelDataModel.md)
  : Plot a RelDataModel object
- [`read_SQL_data_model()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/read_SQL_data_model.md)
  [`readSQLDataModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/read_SQL_data_model.md)
  : Read a data model from an SQL file from the MySQL Workbench
- [`read_json_data_model()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/read_json_data_model.md)
  : Read a data model from JSON
- [`read_named_MM()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/read_named_MM.md)
  : Read a named sparse matrix in MatrixMarket text format
- [`read_named_MM_header()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/read_named_MM_header.md)
  : Read the header of a named sparse matrix in MatrixMarket text format
- [`recover_RelDataModel()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/recover_RelDataModel.md)
  : Recover an autosaved RelDataModel
- [`remove_field()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/remove_field.md)
  : Remove a field from a table in a RelDataModel
- [`remove_foreign_key()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/remove_foreign_key.md)
  : Remove a foreign key between two tables
- [`remove_index()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/remove_index.md)
  : Remove an index from a table in a RelDataModel
- [`remove_table()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/remove_table.md)
  : Remove a table from a RelDataModel
- [`rename_field()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/rename_field.md)
  : Rename an existing field in a RelDataModel table
- [`rename_table()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/rename_table.md)
  : Rename a table in a RelDataModel
- [`set_primary_key()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/set_primary_key.md)
  : Set the primary key a table in a RelDataModel
- [`set_unique_index()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/set_unique_index.md)
  : Set table index uniqueness in a RelDataModel
- [`` `[`( ``*`<RelDataModel>`*`)`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/sub-.RelDataModel.md)
  : Subset a RelDataModel
- [`toDBM()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/toDBM.md) :
  Convert a RelDataModel object in a list of 5 normalized tibbles
- [`update_field()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/update_field.md)
  : Update field information in a table of a RelDataModel
- [`update_foreign_key()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/update_foreign_key.md)
  : Update a the cardinalities of a foreign key between two tables
- [`update_table_display()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/update_table_display.md)
  : Update the display of a table of a RelDataModel
- [`view_confrontation_report()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/view_confrontation_report.md)
  : View confrontation report in rstudio viewer
- [`write_json_data_model()`](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/reference/write_json_data_model.md)
  : Write a data model in a JSON file

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Modeling Relational Data in R with
  ReDaMoR](https://patzaw.github.io/ReDaMoR/articles/ReDaMoR.md):
